overall_justification: While the newspaper reports on market activity and mentions the Convênio de Taubaté, its main editorial stance on the "Caixa de Conversão" leans towards the Orthodox perspective. It criticizes the Convênio and links it to currency devaluation, "descrédito do paiz," and "roubo a propriedade alheia."  The editorial emphasizes the slow and progressive influence of the current exchange rate on prices and suggests that achieving a higher, fixed rate (closer to 27d) would involve significant sacrifices for the Treasury. This aligns with the Orthodox view of prioritizing a strong and stable currency, even at a cost.

supporting_evidence.quote: Os nossos illustres collegas do Jornal do Commercio voltaram hontem a questão do Convênio, questão que para o Jornal não é mais do que a «desvalorisação do nosso meio circulante», o «descrédito do paiz» e um «roubo a propriedade alheia».
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote, from the main editorial on the topic, clearly frames the Convênio (and implicitly the associated "Caixa de Conversão" at a lower rate) in a highly negative light, using terms favored by the Orthodox perspective.

supporting_evidence.quote: Os que esperam a posse de uma taxa a 27 podem confiar que não a conseguirão sem grandes sacrifícios do Thesouro pagando a preço maior o que a menor preço lhe era offerecido...
supporting_evidence.reason_for_inclusion: This quote highlights the potential costs of achieving a higher exchange rate, a concern typically raised by the Orthodox side, while also suggesting that such a rate is ultimately desirable.
